content.full_text:
September 7, 1965

Honorable Dianne Feinstein
Board of Supervisors
City Hall
San Francisco, California  94102

Dear Ms. Feinstein:

     Michael Bennett has asked me to answer your letter of August 23, 1976, regarding the disposition of surplus funds from a 1975 campaign.  The Political Reform Act does not regulate such disposition.  The only reference to surplus funds in the Act appears in Government Code Section 84102(f) which provides that a committee statement of organization must state how surplus funds will be disposed if the committee is dissolved.  The Act, however, does not require the committee to dispose of the funds in the manner stated in the statement of organization.  Therefore, a candidate or committee may dispose of remaining campaign funds in any manner.

     I hope this information is of assistance.

Sincerely,


Nan Hambleton
Special Compliance Representative

NH:bw

bcc:  Jeanne P.

[The image shows a pink/faded document that is difficult to read clearly. Most of the text is reversed/mirrored and very faint. The following can be made out:]

[illegible] "transmitted"

[Large section of body text that is too faded and reversed to read accurately]

"Sincerely"

[illegible] new
[illegible] [illegible] [illegible]

[illegible]

"Dear [illegible]"

[Note: The majority of this document is too faded, reversed, and illegible to transcribe accurately. The pink/magenta coloring and the reversed text make it impossible to read most of the content with confidence.]

MEMBER
Board of Supervisors

DIANNE FEINSTEIN

August 23, 1976

Mr. Michael Bennett
Executive Director
Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street
Sacramento, CA 95814

Dear Mr. Bennett:

This is to inquire as to what general disposition
may be made of surplus funds remaining from a
prior political campaign conducted in 1975.

May I use the money to contribute to other political
campaigns? May I use it for a future political
campaign of mine? May I use the money to remunerate
special staff for civic projects. May I use the
money for charitable contributions?

Your response to the above questions would be greatly
appreciated.

Thank you very much.

Sincerely,

DIANNE FEINSTEIN

DF:bp

[illegible] WIZJSKI

[The document appears to be heavily faded and mostly illegible. The majority of the text is too faint to read with confidence. There are some blue stamp marks or annotations on the left side of the document, but the specific text content cannot be reliably transcribed due to the poor quality and fading of the image.]
